content: The orthographic variant "reflexion" (the older, less common spelling of "reflection" taught in high school vocabulary curricula) can be framed as a built-in self-reflection trigger for large language models: when an LLM encounters the non-standard spelling "reflexion" instead of the modern "reflection", it acts as a natural cue to activate its deliberate self-reflection mechanism, rather than defaulting to fast, unexamined generation.
